title: Prompt Lifecycle, Monitoring, and Production Capstone · Mastery Check
summary: Ship and maintain a cross-provider prompt system with regression evidence. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a production prompt portfolio and release gate. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Store owners, versions, model assumptions, templates, and evaluation links in a prompt registry.
Block prompt releases on material deterministic, rubric, safety, or human-review regressions.
Monitor outcome, refusal, tool, latency, cost, and drift signals without logging sensitive content unnecessarily.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Prompt Registry evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Prompt Registry assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Use a changed quiz case for prompt production capstone that tests this competency: Store owners, versions, model assumptions, templates, and evaluation links in a prompt registry.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Prompt Registry has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Prompt Registry precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed quiz case for prompt production capstone that tests this competency: Store owners, versions, model assumptions, templates, and evaluation links in a prompt registry.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Prompt text can be edited in production without traceability.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints for the quiz Prompt Registry case
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure for the quiz Prompt Registry case

Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise for the quiz Prompt Registry case

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend prompt design choices with evidence across Claude, Codex, changed cases, and limitations.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
